acceptance.command: focused Rust tests plus Spatial camera static gate
acceptance.proof: Every capture-to-present stage names its clock, timestamp semantics, pose source, target time, confidence, and does-not-prove boundary; the sidecar never becomes a second OpenXR frame-loop owner.
Capture-pose association interpolates bracketed samples, bounded presentation targeting extrapolates only within configured limits, and fallbacks remain explicit and tested.

acceptance.command: focused Rust/Kotlin tests and shader/static contract checks
acceptance.proof: Left and right camera streams retain independent static lens pose and intrinsic calibration through shader submission, with per-eye validity and rollback markers.

acceptance.command: serial-scoped Baseline45Control versus presentation-pose A/B receipt
acceptance.proof: Every-available native camera adoption remains the default unless a serial-scoped 45 Hz control produces lower measured pose/hold error and better human motion acceptance without increased frame age.

acceptance.command: serial-scoped device wrapper plus user visual confirmation
acceptance.proof: Automated headset evidence passes and the user confirms that controlled yaw/pitch no longer drags held camera frames at normal and reasonably fast motion; lateral motion is separately labeled according to rotation-only limits.
The prior SensorWarpCameraCalibrated behavior and presentation prediction off mode remain selectable without an APK rebuild.

commit_policy: Keep the plan, focused implementation, tests, and sanitized workflow receipt in one reviewable local Quest slice; do not publish without explicit user authorization.

instruction_surfaces.change_reason: Confirm that presentation-time camera correction remains app-local Quest render adoption and uses existing serial-scoped device policy.
Document the new presentation-pose modes, telemetry, presets, rollback, and human motion protocol.
Route readers to the completed Spatial camera presentation-time remediation and its evidence limits.
Record pose/time authority, per-eye calibration, and the prohibition on a second xrWaitFrame owner.
Replace the old motion A/B instructions with measurable presentation-time and controlled-motion acceptance gates.
Confirm that private effect sampling consumes the public presentation-reprojection ABI without moving private formulas into the public repo.
Document and verify private guide-pass presentation reprojection and resident prewarped camera sampling.
Confirm that the correction does not change public/private routing or reusable-module ownership.
Apply the existing cross-rate timestamp, authority, observability, and validation contracts without changing the skill.
Use the existing Quest lease, APK build, serial-scoped ADB, artifact, and human visual validation policy.
Use pre/post exact-tree graph snapshots to reconcile the bounded camera timing impact surface.

non_scope: Calling xrWaitFrame, xrBeginFrame, or xrEndFrame from the sidecar while Spatial SDK owns the application frame loop.
Claiming compositor scanout or photon time from Vulkan queue-present timestamps.
Publishing private effects, study semantics, device serials, raw logs, captures, APKs, or headset-specific calibration values.
Adding depth-based translational reprojection or inventing scene geometry where only rotation-safe camera correction is available.
Making 45 Hz adoption the production default without controlled evidence that it beats every-available native camera adoption.
objective: Remove the residual rotational camera-frame drag in the Spatial Camera Panel by making capture, pose-sampling, render-target, per-eye calibration, and presentation-call timing explicit; implement the strongest Spatial-SDK-compatible presentation-pose correction; and validate native camera cadence against 45 Hz adoption as a control rather than a presumed fix.
outputs: Checked-in Spatial camera presentation-time remediation plan and acceptance matrix.
Capture-pose interpolation, bounded presentation-pose targeting, per-eye calibration, and timing observability implementation.
Private effect guide ABI that applies the same per-eye correction before resident guide generation.
Hotloadable production candidate, prediction-off rollback, and 45 Hz diagnostic-control presets.
Host/static/build/device receipts with explicit evidence semantics and human visual decision.

validation.command: focused native camera-latency unit tests and Spatial static gates
Spatial Camera Panel validation, debug APK build, and repository diff/graph reconciliation
serial-scoped install/launch, preset sweep, PID-scoped logcat receipt, automated timing assertions, controlled user yaw/pitch/lateral motion, visual decision, and cleanup
